source: Generated from a per-system Morphous manifest and prompt records. CSS variables are shadcn/tweakcn-compatible.
typography: Operational infrastructure typography with clear English labels, sturdy numerals, and readable Japanese UI guidance for dense monitoring surfaces.
layout: Precise 8px grids, angular stone-edge dividers, compact navigation, dashboard-heavy surfaces, stable component states, and restrained granular texture.

prompts.label: Transparent talus boulder motif
prompts.prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: Morphous transparent motif cutout source
Primary request: Create only a compact cluster of alpine talus boulders, isolated full subject, no environment beyond the stones, no props, no text.
Subject: angular talus boulders from an alpine basin, stacked but stable, fractured slate-grey rock faces, cool shadowed crevices, sparse moss green patches and lichen, chipped edges, sediment dust, heavy infrastructure-like mass and stability. The stones should read as talus boulders, not smooth river rocks and not a cliff wall.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; do not crop the subject. Compact low mound silhouette suitable as a motif asset.
Lighting/mood: clean studio light that preserves slate grey, moss green, deep shadow, and rugged mineral texture.
Transparent workflow: place the subject on a perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background for background removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, or lighting variation. Do not use #ff00ff anywhere in the subject.
Constraints: no text, no watermark, no people, no animals, no plants except subtle moss/lichen on the rocks, no landscape horizon, no decorative background, no second subject.

prompts.label: Light-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous light-mode design-system board
Input image: the visible transparent talus boulder motif is the source reference for palette, texture, massing, edge geometry, and mood.
Primary request: Generate a comprehensive light-mode design-system board for "Talus Field", derived from alpine basin talus boulders. It should feel like a stable infrastructure system: durable, load-bearing, calm, operational, and precise.
Reference use: Extract palette, texture rhythm, contrast, spacing mood, and interaction language from the talus motif: slate grey boulder planes, moss green lichen, deep crevice shadow, pale stone dust, angular stacked forms. The board must visibly respect these motif colors.
Canvas: wide 16:9 board, preferred 3840x2160 composition.
Resolution and legibility: high-resolution 4K-class source board. Use large readable labels and avoid dense tiny text.
Required visible sections: title "Talus Field", motif story "alpine basin / talus boulders", palette, typography, Japanese typography, components, navigation, dashboard/data, forms, table, command/search, status states, texture, radius, spacing, shadow, tokens, generated asset examples.
Typography: include English and Japanese examples; choose font guidance for stable infrastructure system UI, clear numerals, calm labels, and dense operational screens.
Style/medium: premium shadcn/tweakcn-style product design system, stable infrastructure system, precise 8px grid, 8px card radius, matte stone-paper surfaces, angular divider rhythm, subtle granular boulder texture accents.
Color palette: use only motif-derived colors plus near-neutrals: glacier wash #f1f3ef, stone panel #d8ddd5, talus grey #7b827f, moss signal #65744b, crevice shadow #303534, boulder ink #171b1a, lichen highlight #aeb88a, cold edge #9ba3a0, dust line #c5c0ad.
Text: use short readable UI labels; avoid long paragraphs.
Constraints: no unrelated bright colors, no generic purple/blue gradients, no marketing hero layout, no random brand names, no watermark, no tiny illegible token tables.

prompts.label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous dark-mode design-system board
Input images: the visible transparent talus boulder motif and the visible light design-system board are references. Preserve the same identity, component language, section coverage, spacing, typography, and motif-derived palette roles.
Primary request: Generate a comprehensive dark-mode design-system board for "Talus Field". It must feel like the same alpine basin talus boulder system translated to dark mode, not a new theme. The system feel is stable infrastructure: durable, load-bearing, calm, operational, and precise.
Reference use: Use talus boulder planes, moss green lichen, deep crevice shadow, pale stone dust, angular stacked forms, and the light board's operational dashboard/component structure.
Canvas: wide 16:9 board, preferred 3840x2160 composition.
Resolution and legibility: high-resolution 4K-class source board. Use large readable labels and avoid dense tiny text.
Required visible sections: title "Talus Field", "Dark Mode", motif story "alpine basin / talus boulders", palette, typography, Japanese typography, components, navigation, dashboard/data, forms, table, command/search, status states, texture, radius, spacing, shadow, tokens, generated asset examples.
Visual details: deep crevice background #121615, boulder ink surface #171b1a, talus panel #252a28, shadow panel #303534, light foreground #f1f3ef, muted text #b6bdb8, moss primary #7f9162, lichen accent #aeb88a, cold edge #9ba3a0, dust separators #4b4e45. Restrained contrast, subtle granular stone texture overlays, angular dividers, crisp shadcn-style cards and controls, 8px radius.
Typography: include English and Japanese examples; clear numerals, calm labels, and dense operational infrastructure screens.
Style/medium: premium shadcn/tweakcn-style product design system, stable infrastructure system, precise 8px grid, dark mode dashboard UI.
Text: use short readable UI labels; avoid long paragraphs.
Constraints: no neon colors, no pure black flat background, no unrelated saturated colors, no generic purple/blue gradients, no marketing hero layout, no random brand names, no watermark, no tiny illegible token tables.
